description_long: This is a vintage wall-mounted corded telephone, primarily constructed from dark-stained wood with a rectangular body and a decorative top edge. The front features two brass bells, a black rotary receiver on a hook, and a circular mouthpiece protruding from the center. A metal crank handle is visible on the right side, used for generating a signal, and a hinged wooden shelf extends from the lower front for writing or note-taking. The left side displays the coiled cord connecting the receiver to the main body, while the back is a plain wooden panel. The overall design is reminiscent of early 20th-century telephones commonly found in homes or small offices.
description: A vintage wall-mounted wooden corded telephone with brass bells, a rotary receiver, and a crank handle. The design features a hinged shelf and a coiled cord connecting the handset.
description_view_0: Front view showing the brass bells, circular mouthpiece, rotary receiver on a hook, and the hinged wooden shelf.
description_view_1: Right side view highlighting the protruding mouthpiece, crank handle, and the thickness of the wooden body.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain wooden back panel with no visible interactive features.
description_view_3: Left side view showing the hanging receiver, coiled cord, and the side profile of the shelf and bells.
